title: Market Day Jamboree!
description: A fun Soca song about going to the market in Antigua and Barbuda, buying fresh fruits and vegetables, and celebrating community spirit.
lyrics:
[Chorus]
Market day, market day, sun is shining bright!
[CLAP]Come let's go, let's go and feel the island light!
[SNAP]Basket ready, money too, for treats so sweet and fine,
[STOMP]Market day Jamboree, a joyful, happy time!

[Verse 1]
Down in St. John's town, vendors line the street,
Mangoes, pineapples, colors can't be beat.
'Wah gwaan?' [What's going on?] they call, with smiles so wide,
Fresh fruits and veggies, bursting with island pride.

[Chorus]
Market day, market day, sun is shining bright!
[CLAP]Come let's go, let's go and feel the island light!
[SNAP]Basket ready, money too, for treats so sweet and fine,
[STOMP]Market day Jamboree, a joyful, happy time!

[Verse 2]
Sweet potatoes, pumpkins, green bananas too,
Everything is fresh, for me and for you.
Red bell peppers, shiny and round,
The best provisions in the whole town are found!

[Chorus]
Market day, market day, sun is shining bright!
[CLAP]Come let's go, let's go and feel the island light!
[SNAP]Basket ready, money too, for treats so sweet and fine,
[STOMP]Market day Jamboree, a joyful, happy time!

[Verse 3]
Listen to the music, steelpan in the air,
Everyone is dancing, without a single care.
Chatting with our neighbors, sharing stories old,
Market day memories, more precious than gold.

[Chorus]
Market day, market day, sun is shining bright!
[CLAP]Come let's go, let's go and feel the island light!
[SNAP]Basket ready, money too, for treats so sweet and fine,
[STOMP]Market day Jamboree, a joyful, happy time!
thumbnailPrompt: A vibrant and colorful illustration depicting a bustling market scene in Antigua and Barbuda. Children are dancing to steelpan music near stalls overflowing with tropical fruits (mangoes, pineapples, bananas) and vegetables (sweet potatoes, pumpkins). Vendors with smiling faces are interacting with customers. The background features brightly painted houses and palm trees under a sunny sky. Incorporate musical notes floating in the air to visually represent the Soca music.
educationalValue: Teaches children about market day traditions in Antigua and Barbuda, the importance of community, and introduces them to common fruits and vegetables found in the Caribbean. Also introduces a Patois phrase.
